title: Police Stop: Calm First Response
situation: An officer stops you and asks what you are doing in the area late at night.
userGoal: Give short factual answers and ask for clarification when needed.
pressureQuestion: Why are you here right now?
contextNotes: Do not argue in this speaking drill.
avoidSaying: I can say anything and leave.
safePhrases: I'm walking home from work on 8th Avenue.
Could you clarify what details you need from me?
betterAnswer: I'm heading home from my shift. I live two blocks away, and I can explain my route if that helps.

title: Minor Traffic Accident: Witness Statement
situation: You witnessed a low-speed bike and car collision and need to explain what you saw.
userGoal: Describe sequence and location clearly without guessing unknown details.
pressureQuestion: What exactly did you see first?
contextNotes: State what you observed, not assumptions.
avoidSaying: I didn't see it, but the driver was wrong.
safePhrases: I saw the bike enter the crosswalk at the green light.
I can't confirm speed, but the impact looked minor.
betterAnswer: First I heard a horn, then I saw the bicycle in the crosswalk and the car braking. I can point to where each person was standing after the impact.
